sections.title: One-sentence summary
sections.text: EV-304 (n=808, ASCO GU 2026) is now the strongest perioperative EV+pembro evidence in cisplatin-eligible MIBC including cN1 M0 — EFS HR 0.53, pCR 55.8% vs 32.5%. EV-ECLIPSE remains the only node-focused prospective EV+pembro trial (~23 patients). Retrospective cN+ NAC data (304-659 patients) supports chemotherapy before RC with 48-56% pN0 rates in cN1.

sections.title: New: EV-304 / KEYNOTE-B15 (cisplatin-eligible)
sections.text: Published February 2026: 808 patients with T2-T4aN0M0 or T1-T4aN1M0 randomized to perioperative EV+pembro vs neoadjuvant gemcitabine/cisplatin. 2-year EFS 79.4% vs 66.2% (HR 0.53); pCR 55.8% vs 32.5%. FDA priority review pending. This directly addresses the prior gap — perioperative EV+pembro in cisplatin-FIT patients with allowed cN1 disease.

sections.title: Best design match for node-positive
sections.text: EV-ECLIPSE is still the only trial designed specifically for node-positive MIBC before cystectomy with EV+pembro (~23 patients, phase 2, no published results). Near-perfect match for cT2 cN1 cM0 with PET-positive external iliac node.

sections.title: Strongest EV+pembro activity data
sections.text: EV-302 lymph-node-only subgroup (n=207): ORR 77.5% vs 53.4%, PFS HR 0.47, OS HR 0.51. Metastatic/unresectable setting — supports biological rationale but not the curative perioperative pathway.

sections.title: Approved perioperative alternative (durvalumab)
sections.text: NIAGARA: 1,063 patients; perioperative durvalumab + GC. FDA approved. cN1 subgroup n=58 with EFS HR 0.75 (NS). 2025 post-hoc: benefit in both pCR and non-pCR patients; DFS HR 0.69 post-RC.

sections.title: If cisplatin-ineligible
sections.text: KEYNOTE-905: perioperative EV+pembro FDA-approved Nov 2025 for cis-ineligible MIBC. EFS HR 0.40, pCR 57.1%. Only ~17 cN1 patients (4.9%).

sections.title: Retrospective cN+ NAC evidence (standard chemo)
sections.text: Zargar-Shoshtar 2016: 304 cN1-N3 patients — 48% pN0 after induction chemo (56% in cN1). Hermans population study (n=659): cN1 3-y OS 66% with NAC vs 37% upfront RC. These underpin the standard NAC-before-RC argument for node-positive disease — but with chemotherapy, not EV+pembro.

sections.title: Foundational RCT comparator
sections.text: SWOG 8710 and BA06 enrolled cN0 only (0 node-positive patients). Meta-analyses support NAC in MIBC broadly; cN+ evidence is retrospective.

sections.title: After surgery
sections.text: If cystectomy pathology shows pN+: CheckMate 274 (143 pN1) and AMBASSADOR support adjuvant immunotherapy.

sections.title: Why nodal response matters
sections.text: PURE-01: 5-y OS only 41.9% for ypN+ vs 89.5% for ypT0N0. JU Open Plus biomarker study: pN0 response predicted survival better than pT0 alone after NAC. Achieving pN0 after systemic therapy is a critical surrogate for cure in cN1 disease.

tumor_board_questions.question: Is she cisplatin-eligible by Galsky criteria? How does that change the choice between NIAGARA/EV-304 vs KEYNOTE-905?
Was the external iliac node biopsy-proven cN1, or imaging-only (PET ≥1.0 cm)? Would EUS-FNA or repeat PET change staging?
Is perioperative EV+pembro (EV-304 data: EFS HR 0.53, pCR 55.8%) available/reimbursable here, or only durvalumab+GC (NIAGARA)?
Is EV-ECLIPSE or another node-positive perioperative trial accessible if standard SOC is chosen first?
What is the realistic pN0 rate with GC/ddMVAC in cN1 (retro data ~56%) vs expected with EV+pembro (EV-304 pCR 55.8% overall)?
What restaging (CT, PET, MRI) is planned after neoadjuvant cycles, and what nodal response would still proceed to cystectomy?
Optimal timing of radical cystectomy + PLND after systemic therapy? Is the external iliac node in the standard PLND template?
If pathology after RC shows persistent pN+: adjuvant nivolumab (CheckMate 274) or pembrolizumab (AMBASSADOR) — eligibility and duration?
How should we weigh EV-302 LN-only efficacy (n=207, ORR 77.5%) — biological rationale only, not perioperative SOC?
NIAGARA cN1 subgroup n=58 had NS benefit (EFS HR 0.75) — is durva+GC still justified in cN1 given EV-304?
If no IO/EV access: is induction GC/ddMVAC the institutional standard for cN1 M0 before RC (Zargar 56% cN1 pN0)?
Document EAU/NCCN-aligned rationale, patient fitness, and shared decision on perioperative regimen before cycle 1.

talking_points_for_oncologist: EV-304 (n=808): perioperative EV+pembro beats GC in cisplatin-eligible MIBC — EFS HR 0.53, pCR 55.8%; includes cN1 M0
EV-ECLIPSE (n≈23): only trial designed for node-positive perioperative EV+pembro — phase 2, no results yet
EV-302 LN-only (n=207): proves EV+pembro activity in nodal disease — metastatic setting
NIAGARA (n=58 cN1): approved durva+GC alternative; cN1 subgroup statistically inconclusive
Zargar-Shoshtar (n=304 cN+): 56% cN1 patients achieve pN0 with induction chemo — standard NAC evidence
SWOG 8710: 0 cN1 patients — foundational but not directly applicable to nodes
CheckMate 274 / AMBASSADOR: adjuvant backup if surgery leaves pN+

ui_copy.site_subtitle: Regionally node-positive muscle-invasive bladder cancer · cN1–N3 M0
ui_copy.scope_banner: This website covers ONE situation only: MIBC with involved pelvic lymph nodes and no distant metastases (cN1–N3 cM0). It does not cover node-negative MIBC, bladder-sparing treatment, or metastatic disease.
ui_copy.scope_hero: For patients whose cancer has spread to regional lymph nodes — but not elsewhere — and who may receive systemic therapy before surgery.
ui_copy.not_this_site: Not for: cN0 M0 localized MIBC · trimodality bladder preservation · M1 metastatic disease
ui_copy.disclaimer: This tool summarizes published clinical trial evidence for educational and decision-support purposes. It is not medical advice. All treatment decisions require qualified oncology consultation. Scope: regionally node-positive MIBC (cN1–N3 cM0) only.
ui_copy.profile_banner: Example profile: pT2 HG · L1 · PET+ external iliac node · cN1 cM0 · perioperative intent
